channel.title: Tampa Bay News Monitor
channel.description: Press releases and breaking news, Tampa Bay

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] New report details just how much a new car will cost you and the reasons why
item.description: New car prices are approaching $50,000 on average. Here's what's driving costs up and what buyers can do about it. 🚗

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] US signs new deal to develop civilian nuclear energy for Saudi Arabia
item.description: The U.S. and Saudi Arabia have signed a new agreement that will give Saudi Arabia access to nuclear enrichment to support a civilian nuclear power program.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Tampa airport joins TSA's new private screening program raising concerns from workers' union
item.description: Tampa International Airport has opted into TSA Gold+, a new public-private partnership that will replace federal Transportation Security Administration workers with a private contractor workforce.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] How arsonists used cats to spread Italy’s wildfires
item.description: As wildfires blazed in southern Italy this week, authorities probing for arson found a grisly clue: cats “with flammable-soaked rags tied to their tails.”

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] House Republicans pass $95B budget to fund Iran war, Trump priorities
item.description: The House adopted a Republican-only $95 billion budget proposal Wednesday, a long-shot effort to fund the Iran war and other White House priorities.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Pinellas Park homeowners could see drop in flood insurance cost
item.description: The City of Pinellas Park was just classified as Class 5 for FEMA's National Flood Insurance Premium Community Rating System.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Collisions in space are a growing concern. A plan to address it is reigniting a turf war
item.description: The FCC unanimously adopted new space traffic rules requiring real-time satellite tracking — but a congressional debate over whether the agency has authority to regulate space safety is far from over.

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] Soaring utility costs impact Tampa residents during peak summer heat: 'I was shocked'
item.description: Tampa residents say their monthly electric bills have doubled or tripled as the summer heat forces air conditioners to work overtime.

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] Hillsborough school board considers turning former McLane Middle School campus into workforce housing
item.description: District leaders are considering turning part of the former McLane Middle School campus into workforce housing to help Brandon school employees find affordable homes.

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] Woman files negligence lawsuit in Sarasota County after father found dead in apartment
item.description: A Michigan woman has filed a civil negligence lawsuit in Sarasota County against a security guard and his firm, alleging they ignored signs of distress before her father was found dead inside his apartment.

item.title: [ABC7 Suncoast (WWSB)] Sarasota County School Board votes to move regular meetings back to 6 p.m.

[ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Tampa Bay Sports Commission discussed upcoming events on Denis Phillips Live
item.description: The Executive Director of the Tampa Bay Sports Commission stopped by DPL to talk about the match and other exciting sports events they have coming up in Tampa Bay.

item.title: [BREAKING] [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] St. Pete shooting: 2 injured, investigation underway at Childs Park YMCA
item.description: A shooting investigation is underway at the Childs Park YMCA in St. Petersburg.

[ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] South Tampa mosquito spraying planned overnight Thursday: Hillsborough officials
item.description: Hillsborough County mosquito management crews plan to conduct overnight mosquito treatments across parts of the area by spraying Dibrom between 3:30 a.m. and 6 a.m.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Chiefs release first renderings of $3B domed stadium in Kansas City, Kansas
item.description: The Kansas City Chiefs released renderings on Wednesday of what their new domed stadium in Kansas will look like.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Florida star RB Jadan Baugh explains why he stayed in Gainesville after coaching change
item.description: The All-SEC running back said a Christmas Day visit from new head coach Jon Sumrall was the moment that convinced him to remain a Florida Gator.

item.title: [BREAKING] [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] 2 injured after shooting inside Childs Park YMCA bathroom in St. Pete, police say
item.description: Police said two people are injured following a shooting inside a bathroom at the Childs Park YMCA in St. Petersburg.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] US attorney who Trump fired minutes after he got the job sues to get it back
item.description: A fired U.S. attorney is suing the Trump administration after being fired less than an hour after judges unanimously appointed him to the role.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Trump pays respects to 4 US service members killed in the Middle East
item.description: President Donald Trump on Wednesday honored the lives of four U.S. service members who were killed in the Middle East and returned to their families at an air force base in Delaware.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Tampa Bay 28's Denis Phillips to meet fans at Bolts Brew Fest this Saturday
item.description: The Tampa Bay Lightning are hosting their annual Bolts Brew Fest on Saturday, July 25, at Benchmark International Arena.

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] Tampa pharmacist found guilty of illegally dispensing more than 335,000 oxycodone pills: DOJ
item.description: A federal jury convicted a Tampa pharmacist and pharmacy owner of illegally distributing more than 335,000 high-dose oxycodone pills through two South Florida pharmacies, federal prosecutors announced.

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] St. Petersburg police search for suspect after city worker, mother of 4 shot and killed
item.description: Investigators are searching for a suspect after a 47-year-old mother working for the city of St. Petersburg was shot and killed Wednesday in what officials believe was a targeted, possibly domestic, attack.

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] Polk County man accused of having sex with minor he brought home from Pennsylvania: 'He loved her'
item.description: A Polk City man was arrested Tuesday after being accused of having unlawful sex with a 16-year-old, among other charges, according to the sheriff’s office.

item.title: [ABC7 Suncoast (WWSB)] Florida’s new state budget funds five major Manatee County projects

item.title: [The Florida Channel] 7/21/26 Capitol Update Extended – State Board of Immigration Enforcement Awards Reimbursements to Law Enforcement Agencies Throughout Florida
item.description: 7/21/26 Capitol Update Extended – State Board of Immigration Enforcement Awards Reimbursements to Law Enforcement Agencies Throughout Florida first appeared on The Florida Channel State CFO Blaise Ingoglia awarded reimbursements on behalf of the State Board of Immigration Enforcement. The board is comprised of the governor and cabinet and was created to ensure enforcement of federal immigration po
